markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Document tools, primitives, APIs, backend navigation commands, visual-debug surfaces, and safe usage patterns. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualToolPrimitiveCatalog leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualToolPrimitiveCatalog is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 fcf9bce3. Machine-readable tool/primitive catalog in user_manual_tool_entries: 99 wp009_surface rows generated FROM the registry (method, route, summary, expected input/output, per-group common_errors + recovery_steps - catalog cannot drift from the declared inventory) PLUS the full legacy static MODEL_MANUAL command_reference imported deterministically (100+ rows, origin legacy_model_manual, content preserved exactly incl. ipc_channel/tauri_command/cli_flag/schema_fields/common_errors/recovery_steps - the spec 10.15.8 deterministic mapping). Feature entries: one per WP-009 surface group + every legacy feature group. Surface-guide pages seeded per group (knowledge-index incl. code-nav, notes-loom, rich-documents, retrieval, memory, crdt, usermanual) each with route tables (markdown + machine-readable body_json), inputs/outputs, failure modes, recovery, and visual-debug hooks (GET /knowledge/memory/visual-debug + the manual HTML projection selectors). THIS CLOSES THE MT-112 DEFERRAL: all 6 /knowledge/code/* nav routes are manual-registered (anchors + tool entries) - see MT-112.json lifecycle.manual_registration_closure. Tests GREEN on real PG: lib tool_catalog_covers_registry_and_legacy_without_id_collisions, user_manual_api_tests::mt201_tools_list_and_read_resolve (per-route entries readable incl. every /knowledge/code/* row with errors+recovery; legacy origin filter returns the imported manifest), mtdoc_every_registry_surface_exists_on_the_real_router (each cataloged route probed on the live router), user_manual_storage_tests::mt195_every_registry_surface_has_db_coverage (tool entry per surface in DB).

concerns_found_and_fixed: Catalog-drift risk (hand-written per-route docs rot): tool entries derive from the registry constant at seed time, and the registry is triply gated (compile/DB/runtime) - a route description cannot exist without its surface and vice versa.
Legacy/new id collision space checked: lib test asserts zero tool_id collisions between dotted registry ids and snake_case legacy ids.
residual_risks: Per-group (not per-route) error/recovery vocabularies trade granularity for accuracy - each listed code was live-triggered or grep-verified against the module's error constructors; per-route refinement is cheap later because rows are hash-resynced.

adversarial_review_v2.summary: Follow-up adversarial review found no blocking findings after durable ModelManual-to-UserManual wording, USER_MANUAL_VERSION HBR-MAN-001 guidance, Loom route parity, full-row freshness, receipt atomicity, and fail-closed PostgreSQL test fixes.

ready_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 working tree after fcf9bce3 includes UserManual recovery fixes; validator must review working-tree diff, not only fcf9bce3.

hardening_closed.note: State recovered to READY_FOR_VALIDATION only. completed_by remains null; validator owns PASS/COMPLETED.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-197 requires a machine-readable UserManual tool/primitive catalog documenting tools, primitives, routes, APIs, common errors and recovery with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority and validator-readable evidence. The implementation builds a deterministic catalog from the WP-009 surface registry plus the full legacy ModelManual manifest, persists it to the real user_manual_tool_entries table, and proves it with real-PostgreSQL tests including a live-router doc-vs-runtime probe — it satisfies the MT and aligns with current Master Spec 10.15.8 / LAW-CORPUS-PARITY-003.
findings: seed.rs:1610-1711 seed_tool_entries() builds the catalog deterministically: one wp009_surface row per registry entry (surface_id, method, route, summary, expected input/output, per-group common_errors + recovery_steps) PLUS every legacy CommandReference imported as origin=legacy_model_manual with content preserved (ipc_channel/tauri_command/cli_flag/schema_fields). Catalog is derived from the registry constant so it cannot drift from the served inventory.

store.rs:704-755 persists tool entries via real INSERT ... ON CONFLICT into the PostgreSQL table user_manual_tool_entries; store.rs:764+ reads via real SELECT. migrations/0310_user_manual.sql:118 CREATE TABLE user_manual_tool_entries with route index — this is real durable PostgreSQL authority, satisfying sub_rule_2.

Tests touch real PG, not mocks: user_manual_support.rs:97-105 app_state_for() connects a real PostgresDatabase + PgPool to an isolated schema; skip_if_no_pg! (support.rs:132-146) PANICS (fails) when PG is unavailable rather than silently skipping, so a green run is real-PG proof not a mock pass.

user_manual_api_tests.rs:212 mt201_tools_list_and_read_resolve hits live HTTP /usermanual/tools endpoints against the mounted router, asserts one wp009 entry per registry surface, reads every /knowledge/code/* entry (errors+recovery non-empty), 404 on missing, and legacy origin returns >50 imported rows. user_manual_api_tests.rs:698 mtdoc_every_registry_surface_exists_on_the_real_router probes every documented surface against the real api::routes router (fails on bare-404 or 405) — a genuine doc-vs-runtime keystone.

seed.rs:1967 tool_catalog_covers_registry_and_legacy_without_id_collisions asserts zero tool_id collisions between dotted registry ids and snake_case legacy ids and full coverage of both sources — addresses the legacy/new id-collision risk.

No deferred-live escapes: grep of src/user_manual/ found no reachable todo!()/unimplemented!()/stub/placeholder; all 'Unavailable'/'SQLite' hits are documentation prose describing fail-closed behavior (seed.rs:288,450,1132 etc.), not code escapes. No SQLite/rusqlite code path exists.

Spec alignment: master-spec-v02.194 10.15.8 (UserManual migration bridge) requires legacy ModelManual to map deterministically to UserManual authority entries; LAW-CORPUS-PARITY-003 (line 8454) requires the catalog be machine-readable backend authority with views as projections only — the implementation matches both.

Aside (non-blocking): MT lifecycle.completed_by is null and the verdict is owned by Integration Validator; evidence references working-tree state at fcf9bce3 while HEAD is now MT-264, but the cited code/tests are present and intact in the current tree.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
